current.title: Skyvern
current.headline: AI agents that drive your browser. 21,000 stars of Selenium-killer.
current.summary: Skyvern automates browser-based workflows with vision-language models — point it at a page, describe the goal, and the agent navigates, fills forms, handles 2FA, and extracts data. Where Selenium and Playwright break the moment a page redesigns, Skyvern adapts because it sees the page like a human would. The CLI skill exposes Skyvern's task runner inside Claude Code: navigate, fill forms, extract data, handle logins, and stream back structured results. Pulled into the marketplace via daily external sync from Skyvern-AI/skyvern (AGPL-3.0). Sourced into the catalog by community contributor @mark1ian.
current.quote: Automate browser-based workflows using LLMs and computer vision.
current.author: Skyvern-AI

previous.title: Code Cleanup
previous.headline: 11-dimensional codebase cleanup with confidence scoring and build verification gates.
previous.summary: Born from a real cleanup session — 25,000 lines removed, 4 bugs caught — packaged into a reusable plugin. 11 specialized agents scan for dead code, AI slop, weak types, security vulnerabilities, N+1 queries, floating promises, circular deps, and more. Every finding gets a confidence score (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). LOW-risk dimensions auto-apply after build verification. HIGH-risk dimensions flag only — never touch your code without proof. One command, full codebase hygiene.
previous.quote: 8 parallel agents, 25K lines removed, 4 bugs found. Now it's a plugin anyone can run.
previous.author: Jeremy Longshore

previous.title: Web Analytics
previous.headline: Your dashboards don't talk. This skill does.
previous.summary: A push-based analytics intelligence team — 9 specialist agents that fetch your data, detect anomalies, analyze funnels, verify claims, and deliver narrative reports. Powered by self-hosted Umami via MCP — agents call the Umami API in real time across your entire site portfolio. GA4 as fallback. Three tiers: /analytics for a 30-second pulse, /analytics medium for a daily brief, /analytics full for a deep cross-site dive with adversarial verification.
previous.quote: I stopped checking dashboards. Now my analytics team wakes me up when something matters.
previous.author: Jeremy Longshore

previous.title: Token Optimizer
previous.headline: Ghost tokens eat your context. Find them. Fix them. Prevent rot.
previous.quote: My Claude Code kept leaking tokens. So I built this. Now my limits last longer, context stays fresh, and I have full visibility and control over my usage & setup.
previous.author: Alex Greenshpun
